description_long: This is a military-style utility helicopter with a streamlined, olive-green fuselage and a prominent main rotor with four long, black blades. The helicopter features a tapered nose with a multi-pane cockpit window, side doors, and a tail boom ending in a small tail rotor. The landing gear consists of two robust skids attached to the underside of the fuselage, providing stability on the ground. The cockpit windows are angular and wrap around the front, offering wide visibility for pilots. The surface finish is matte, with minimal visible markings or insignia, and the overall build suggests a focus on durability and function. No visible registration numbers or civilian markings are present.
description: A matte olive-green military-style helicopter with four main rotor blades, angular cockpit windows, and sturdy landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the tapered nose, angular cockpit windows, and the symmetrical spread of the main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, side doors, landing skids, and the tail boom with the tail rotor visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids beneath the fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the cockpit windows and rotor assembly.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of composite material, colored black, with no visible markings. The blades are evenly spaced and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small tail rotor with four blades, mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored black with no visible markings or protective shrouds.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, tubular landing skids made of metal, colored dark gray or black, attached to the underside of the fuselage with angled supports and crossbars for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple angular cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, made of tinted glass with visible framing, providing wide visibility for the pilots.
